Reviewing representative UI files and architecture docs to ground the modernization plan in what's actually in the codebase.

[REDACTED]

Two decisions will shape the plan most: how far you're willing to go on architecture, and whether the visual direction should stay dark-only or add a light theme.

[REDACTED]

questions.prompt: How far should we go architecturally? (This changes effort and risk a lot.)

options.label: Design-system polish first — keep hybrid React+vanilla, improve tokens, components, mobile, a11y (~2–4 weeks)
Expand React gradually — board first, then admin/apply/company; retire innerHTML templates (~1–2 months)
Full frontend rewrite — single React SPA with shared layout/router (~2–3 months)
Refine dark theme only (current direction, polished)
Dark + light mode with system preference
Dark only, but with a stronger brand identity (custom palette, wordmark, etc.)

content.text:
Sizing the vanilla JS surface area for the full SPA rewrite.

[REDACTED]

You chose a full SPA rewrite with dark and light themes — I'll shape the plan around that scope and the current hybrid stack.

[REDACTED]

input.name: Full SPA UI Modernization
input.overview: Replace the hybrid static-HTML + vanilla JS + partial React panel with a single React SPA (Vite), a proper design system with dark/light themes, and mobile-first responsive layouts—while keeping Flask as a JSON API with an SPA fallback route.
input.plan:
# Full SPA UI Modernization Plan

## Current state (baseline)

The panel is a **multi-page hybrid**: four static HTML shells ([`index.html`](relocation_jobs/static/index.html), [`company.html`](relocation_jobs/static/company.html), [`apply.html`](relocation_jobs/static/apply.html), [`admin.html`](relocation_jobs/static/admin.html)) share one ~6,000-line [`styles.css`](relocation_jobs/static/styles.css). React ([`frontend/`](frontend/)) only renders the job board cards, pagination, and fetch UI on `/`; everything else is ~8,500 lines of vanilla ES modules under [`static/js/`](relocation_jobs/static/js/).

The hardest coupling today is the **React ↔ vanilla bridge**: React renders presentational DOM with `data-*` attributes; [`events.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/events.js) (~865 lines) handles clicks, popovers, and optimistic updates globally. A full SPA removes this split entirely.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph today [Today]
    HTML[4 HTML shells]
    VAN[static/js ~29 modules]
    REACT[frontend React island]
    CSS[styles.css monolith]
    HTML --> VAN
    HTML --> REACT
    VAN <-->|window.relocationJobs| REACT
    VAN --> API
    REACT --> API
  end

  subgraph target [Target]
    SPA[Single React SPA]
    DS[Design system + themes]
    HOOKS[API hooks + client state]
    SPA --> DS
    SPA --> HOOKS
    HOOKS --> API2[Flask /api/*]
  end
```

---

## Recommended stack

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|-------|--------|-----|
| **Router** | React Router v7 | Client routes mirror today: `/`, `/company/:country/:slug`, `/apply`, `/admin` |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS v4 + CSS variables | Token-based theming, fast iteration, small per-component surface |
| **Primitives** | shadcn/ui (Radix) | Professional defaults: Dialog, Sheet, Select, Dropdown, Toast, Tabs—built-in a11y |
| **Server state** | TanStack Query | Board pagination, admin tables, fetch runs—caching, retries, loading/error states |
| **Client state** | Zustand (light) | Auth session, filter prefs (today in [`storage.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/storage.js)), theme choice |
| **Icons** | lucide-react | Replace duplicated inline SVGs |
| **Build** | Expand existing Vite setup | Already outputs to [`relocation_jobs/static/dist/`](relocation_jobs/static/dist/) |

**Not recommended now:** TypeScript (adds migration friction for ~10k LOC move), full component-library swap mid-migration, or changing API contracts.

---

## Design direction (professional + mobile)

### Visual language
- **Typography scale:** 12 / 14 / 16 / 20 / 24px with consistent line-height; keep Inter for UI, JetBrains Mono for slugs/code.
- **Spacing scale:** 4px base (4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48)—replace ad-hoc `0.45rem` / `0.65rem` scattered in CSS.
- **Surfaces:** 3 elevation levels (page bg → card → popover) with subtle borders, not heavy shadows.
- **Color:** Semantic tokens (`background`, `foreground`, `muted`, `accent`, `success`, `warn`, `danger`, `visa`, `referral`) mapped for **both** themes; eliminate hardcoded hex in badge variants.
- **Density:** Comfortable default on desktop; slightly roomier touch targets on `pointer: coarse`.

### Dark + light theme
- `data-theme="dark" | "light"` on `<html>`, default from `prefers-color-scheme`, override stored in `localStorage`.
- Theme toggle in account menu; no flash on load via inline script in SPA shell (standard pattern).
- Test contrast for badges, ATS rings, and referral/visa chips in both modes.

### Mobile-first layout
- **Shared AppShell:** sticky top bar, safe-area padding (`env(safe-area-inset-*)`), max-width content column (1080px board, 1280px workspace).
- **Board filters:** desktop = inline toolbar; mobile = filter **Sheet** (bottom) with chip summary row—replace manual `lockBodyScroll` + backdrop logic.
- **Job actions:** 44px min touch targets; action row wraps to 2-col grid at `md`, single column at `sm`.
- **Company workspace:** sidebar list + detail pane → stacked layout below `lg` (today breaks at 960px).
- **Admin tables:** horizontal scroll container + sticky first column on small screens; card-list fallback optional for phase 2.

### UX polish (what makes it feel “production”)
- Consistent loading: skeleton components per page (board already has [`BoardSkeleton.jsx`](frontend/src/BoardSkeleton.jsx)—extend pattern).
- Empty states with clear next action (e.g. “No roles — fetch this company”).
- Optimistic updates stay (today in [`job-board.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/job-board.js)) but live inside React mutation hooks.
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: disable sheet slide, loader ring animation.
- Focus management on every Dialog/Sheet; `focus-visible` rings on all interactive elements.

---

## Architecture changes

### Frontend structure (new)

```
frontend/src/
  app/           App.jsx, router, providers (Query, theme, auth)
  layouts/       AppShell, AuthGate
  pages/         BoardPage, CompanyPage, ApplyPage, AdminPage
  features/      board/, company/, apply/, admin/, fetch/, auth/
  components/ui/ shadcn primitives (Button, Badge, Dialog, Sheet, …)
  lib/           api client (port from api.js), query keys, formatters
  hooks/         useBoard, useJobMutations, useTheme, …
  styles/        globals.css (theme tokens), tailwind config
```

### Flask (minimal)

In [`relocation_jobs/web/server.py`](relocation_jobs/web/server.py):
- Serve **one** SPA shell (`index.html` or `spa.html`) for `/`, `/admin`, `/apply`, `/company/<country>/<path:slug>`.
- Keep `/static/dist/*` for built assets; retire per-page HTML routes once migrated.
- API routes unchanged.

### Delete after cutover
- [`relocation_jobs/static/*.html`](relocation_jobs/static/) (except SPA shell)
- [`relocation_jobs/static/js/*`](relocation_jobs/static/js/) (entire vanilla layer)
- Monolithic [`styles.css`](relocation_jobs/static/styles.css)—replaced by Tailwind + token file
- `window.relocationJobs` bridge ([`board-sync.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/board-sync.js), [`fetch-sync.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/fetch-sync.js))

---

## Phased migration (~8–10 weeks)

Work **page-by-page** behind the new shell so production stays usable. Each phase ends with a deployable state.

### Phase 0 — Foundation (week 1–2)
- Add Tailwind, shadcn/ui, React Router, TanStack Query, lucide-react to [`frontend/package.json`](frontend/package.json).
- Create theme tokens (dark/light) in `globals.css`; build `AppShell` with header, user menu, theme toggle, nav links.
- Port [`api.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/api.js) → `lib/api.js` + typed-ish fetch wrapper (keep same endpoints).
- Port [`auth.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/auth.js) → `AuthGate` + login form using shadcn inputs.
- Flask SPA fallback route; Vite dev proxy to `:5051` for local work.
- **Deliverable:** authenticated shell with theme toggle; placeholder routes.

### Phase 1 — Job board (week 3–5) — highest traffic
Migrate the core workflow end-to-end:

| Today | Target |
|-------|--------|
| [`filters.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/filters.js) + HTML toolbar | `BoardToolbar` with Select + filter Sheet |
| [`board.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/board.js) + [`board-view.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/board-view.js) | `useBoardQuery` + pagination state |
| [`events.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/events.js) delegated clicks | `useJobMutations` in [`JobCard`](frontend/src/JobCard.jsx) / [`CompanyCard`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx) |
| [`dialogs.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/dialogs.js) (1,154 lines) | `AddCompanyDialog`, `EditCareersDialog`, `EditCityDialog` components |
| [`FetchPanel.jsx`](frontend/src/FetchPanel.jsx) + [`scrape.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/scrape.js) | `useFetchRun` hook; single fetch UI (delete admin duplicate) |
| [`job-board.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/job-board.js) optimistic patches | TanStack Query `onMutate` / `setQueryData` |

Refactor existing React components to use shadcn primitives and **own their interactions** (no `data-action` delegation).

**Deliverable:** `/` fully functional in SPA; legacy board JS can be removed.

### Phase 2 — Company workspace (week 6–7)
Port [`company.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/company.js) (~621 lines):
- Position list sidebar, LaTeX editor, PDF iframe preview, MCP reframe actions.
- Route: `/company/:country/:slug`.
- Reuse board formatters from `lib/format.js` (merge duplicate [`companyWorkspace.js`](frontend/src/companyWorkspace.js)).

**Deliverable:** CV tailoring workflow in SPA.

### Phase 3 — Application data (week 8)
Port [`apply.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/apply.js):
- Profile form, master resume editor, pipeline prompts.
- Form validation + autosave patterns with React Hook Form (optional but helps).

**Deliverable:** `/apply` in SPA.

### Phase 4 — Admin (week 9–10)
Port [`admin.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin.js), [`admin-worker.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin-worker.js), [`stats-dashboard.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/stats-dashboard.js):
- Catalog table, users, fetch config, worker status.
- Reuse fetch panel from Phase 1 (no second implementation).

**Deliverable:** `/admin` in SPA; delete all legacy static JS/HTML.

### Phase 5 — Polish & hardening (ongoing, final week)
- Accessibility audit (keyboard, screen reader labels, focus traps).
- Mobile QA on real devices (iOS Safari safe areas, bottom sheets).
- Route-based code splitting (`React.lazy` per page).
- Bundle size check; tree-shake lucide icons.
- Update [`docs/reference/architecture.md`](docs/reference/architecture.md) and deploy script if entry HTML name changes.

---

## Key migration risks and mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| **events.js behavior regression** | Migrate action-by-action (apply, reject, ATS, pin, hide-reason); keep a short checklist mapped to [`business-rules.md`](docs/reference/business-rules.md) |
| **Optimistic board updates** | Port `patchJobOnBoard` logic into query cache helpers before deleting `job-board.js` |
| **Fetch polling complexity** | Isolate in `useFetchPolling` hook with tests against mock SSE/poll responses |
| **6k-line CSS delete** | Do not translate line-by-line; rebuild screens with shadcn + tokens, port only domain-specific bits (ATS ring, visa badges) |
| **Long admin migration** | Admin last—board + company deliver 90% of daily use |
| **Deploy breakage** | Flask serves new SPA shell first; keep old HTML behind feature flag for one release if needed |

---

## What “done” looks like

- One Vite build → [`relocation_jobs/static/dist/`](relocation_jobs/static/dist/).
- Four routes in React Router; shared header/nav on every page.
- Dark + light themes with system default + manual override.
- Mobile: usable board filtering, job actions, and company workspace without horizontal scroll pain.
- No `window.relocationJobs`, no `events.js`, no duplicate fetch panel.
- API and business rules unchanged; pytest suite still green.

---

## Suggested first PR (small, reviewable)

1. Tooling + theme tokens + `AppShell` + Flask SPA fallback.
2. Login/auth gate working against existing `/api/auth/*`.
3. Board route renders existing React `App` inside new layout (still read-only or partial)—proves the integration before deleting vanilla code.

This gives you something visible on day one without a big-bang rewrite.

todos.content: Phase 0: Add Tailwind + shadcn + Router + Query; theme tokens (dark/light); AppShell; port api.js/auth.js; Flask SPA fallback
Phase 1: Migrate full job board — filters, dialogs, events.js actions, fetch panel, optimistic mutations into React hooks
Phase 2: Migrate company workspace (LaTeX editor, PDF preview, position list) to /company/:country/:slug
Phase 3: Migrate /apply page (profile + master resume editor)
Phase 4: Migrate /admin (catalog, users, fetch worker, stats); delete duplicate fetch UI
Phase 5: Mobile QA, a11y audit, code splitting, delete legacy static/js + styles.css + old HTML shells; update docs

content.text:
<timestamp>Wednesday, Jul 8, 2026, 5:58 AM (UTC+4)</timestamp>
<user_query>
do you think that without any tests on this current front we can move forward?
</user_query>

Checking what frontend-related test coverage exists so the recommendation is grounded in the repo.

[REDACTED]

Yes — you can move forward without retroactively testing the current frontend. I would not block the SPA rewrite on that.

## Why that's reasonable

**The existing front is throwaway.** ~8,500 lines of vanilla JS plus the React↔vanilla bridge (`events.js`, `window.relocationJobs`) are exactly what you're deleting. Writing Vitest/Playwright coverage for that hybrid layer is high effort and low lasting value.

**You already have backend coverage where it matters most.** Pytest covers panel flattening ([`tests/panel/test_preview_board.py`](tests/panel/test_preview_board.py)), API route contracts ([`tests/test_route_manifest.py`](tests/test_route_manifest.py)), catalog, fetch, etc. The server enforces job buckets and business rules — the UI mostly reflects that.

**The rewrite improves testability.** Today, behavior lives in delegated DOM clicks on React output. In the SPA, mutations move into hooks/components — that's actually where tests become worthwhile.

## What I'd worry about without any front tests

Not "will buttons look right" — manual QA covers that. The real regression risks are:

| Area | Risk |
|------|------|
| Optimistic board updates | Apply/reject/not-for-me/pin patches wrong company or wrong bucket |
| Pagination + filters | Page offset resets, filter chips desync from query |
| Fetch polling | Progress UI stuck or double-polling |
| Auth gate | Session lost on navigation in SPA |
| Company workspace | LaTeX save / PDF preview broken after route change |

Those are behavioral, not visual. You'd catch them by using the app daily, not by snapshotting CSS.

## What I'd add during the migration (not before)

Don't backfill legacy front tests. Add a **thin safety net as you build the new stack**:

1. **Vitest** for pure logic ported from [`format.js`](frontend/src/format.js), [`sort.js`](frontend/src/sort.js), and new query-cache helpers (optimistic patch functions). Cheap, fast, high ROI.

2. **3–5 Playwright smoke tests** once Phase 1 board lands:
   - Login → board loads with companies
   - Toggle applied on a job → persists after reload
   - Filter + paginate → correct page content
   - (Optional) Company workspace opens and shows positions

3. **Keep pytest green** after Flask SPA fallback changes — route manifest test already guards API paths.

That's maybe 1–2 days of work spread across Phase 0–1, not a precondition.

## My recommendation

| Approach | Verdict |
|----------|---------|
| Block rewrite until legacy front is fully tested | No — wasted work |
| Rewrite with zero tests, manual QA only | Risky but acceptable for a personal tool you use daily |
| Rewrite + minimal tests on **new** code as you go | Best trade-off |

Given you vibe-coded this fast and now want production quality: **start the rewrite, add tests on the new architecture from Phase 1 onward.** The current front's lack of tests is a reason the rewrite is hard to change safely today — it's not a reason to delay replacing it.

One caveat from your own collaboration norms: if there are known **reliability** problems on the backend (board load incidents, fetch pipeline issues), a 2–3 month UI rewrite is a big parallel track. Worth being clear that UI modernization doesn't fix those — but it doesn't require front tests to begin either.

Want me to add a **Testing strategy** section to the plan (Vitest scope + Playwright smoke list + what explicitly not to test)?

[REDACTED]

<timestamp>Wednesday, Jul 8, 2026, 5:59 AM (UTC+4)</timestamp>

write down your proposal on this big change in our proposals files folder then i will ask you another thing.
</user_query>

[REDACTED]

[REDACTED]

input.contents:
# Full SPA UI modernization — proposal

**Status:** proposal (not approved)  
**Last updated:** 2026-07-08  
**Authors:** architecture discussion (agent + owner review pending)

Related: [architecture.md](architecture.md), [board.md](board.md), [business-rules.md](business-rules.md), [company-workspace.md](company-workspace.md), [rules.md](rules.md), [board-read-model-proposal.md](board-read-model-proposal.md)

---

## Summary

The panel UI is a **hybrid multi-page app**: four static HTML shells, ~8,500 lines of vanilla ES modules, and a partial React island on the main board only. Styling is a single ~6,000-line `styles.css`. React and vanilla JS communicate through `window.relocationJobs` and delegated DOM clicks in `events.js` (~865 lines).

**Recommended direction (default):** replace the hybrid front with a **single React SPA** (Vite), shared **AppShell** layout, **React Router** for all four views, a **design system** (Tailwind + shadcn/ui), **dark + light themes** with system preference, and **mobile-first** responsive patterns. Flask stays a JSON API; add an SPA fallback route. Migrate **page-by-page** over ~8–10 weeks; delete legacy `static/js/`, per-page HTML, and monolithic CSS after cutover.

**Testing stance:** do **not** retroactively test the legacy hybrid front (throwaway code). Add a **thin safety net on the new stack** during migration: Vitest for pure helpers + optimistic cache logic; 3–5 Playwright smoke tests after Phase 1; keep existing pytest API/board tests green.

**Not recommended now:** TypeScript mid-migration; Tailwind line-by-line port of `styles.css`; changing API contracts; blocking the rewrite on legacy front test coverage.

**Decision needed:** approve phased SPA rewrite vs smaller incremental polish (design tokens only, keep hybrid) — see [Decision: scope](#decision-scope).

---

## Problem statement

### What the UI is today

```text
Flask (relocation_jobs/web/server.py)
  → static HTML shells (index, company, apply, admin)
  → static/js/ (~29 ES modules) — auth, filters, dialogs, board logic, admin, apply, company
  → frontend/ (Vite + React 19) — board cards, pagination, fetch panel only on /
  → static/styles.css (~6k lines, dark theme only)
  → /api/* JSON
```

| Route | Shell | UI stack |
|-------|-------|----------|
| `/` | `index.html` | Vanilla + React island |
| `/company/<country>/<slug>` | `company.html` | 100% vanilla JS |
| `/apply` | `apply.html` | 100% vanilla JS |
| `/admin` | `admin.html` | 100% vanilla JS |

React mounts via portals into holes in `index.html` (`#jobs`, `#board-pagination-root`, `#fetch-header-root`, `#fetch-panel-root`). Vanilla owns header, filters, search, sort, dialogs, auth, and all event handling. State crosses the boundary through `board-sync.js`, `fetch-sync.js`, and `window.relocationJobs`.

### Pain points

1. **Two rendering paradigms** — React components are presentational; behavior lives in `events.js` global click delegation. Hard to reason about, test, or extend.
2. **Monolithic CSS** — ~6,000 lines, no spacing/type scale tokens, hardcoded hex mixed with CSS variables, high risk of regressions on any edit.
3. **Duplication** — fetch panel exists in React (`FetchPanel.jsx`) and admin HTML (`admin-worker.js`); `companyWorkspacePath()` duplicated in React and vanilla.
4. **No shared chrome** — each page reloads; header/nav not DRY across four shells.
5. **Dark theme only** — no light mode or `prefers-color-scheme` support.
6. **Mobile patterns are manual** — bottom sheets, scroll lock, and popover positioning implemented ad hoc in vanilla JS.
7. **No frontend tests** — zero Vitest/Playwright; pytest covers API and panel flatten logic only.

### What does *not* need to change

| Area | Why leave it |
|------|--------------|
| Flask `/api/*` contracts | SPA consumes same endpoints; business rules stay server-side |
| Postgres catalog + tracking | Source of truth unchanged |
| `panel/flatten.py` board logic | Server read model; see [board-read-model-proposal.md](board-read-model-proposal.md) for performance work (orthogonal) |
| MCP / fetch worker backends | UI rewrite does not replace fetch pipeline |

---

## Options considered

### A. Design-system polish only (keep hybrid)

Improve `styles.css` tokens, unify buttons/badges, tighten mobile breakpoints. Keep React island + vanilla JS bridge.

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Lowest risk, ~2–4 weeks | Does not fix `events.js` coupling or four-page duplication |
| No Flask routing change | Still two paradigms; harder to hire/contribute |
| | Light theme still awkward across 6k-line CSS |

### B. Expand React gradually (recommended intermediate if SPA feels too big)

Migrate board → company → apply → admin into React behind existing HTML shells; retire vanilla page by page.

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Deployable increments | Still multi-page until router lands |
| Proves React patterns before full cutover | Temporary dual stack longer than full SPA shell-first |

### C. Full SPA rewrite (recommended default)

Single Vite build, React Router, shared AppShell, delete vanilla layer after migration.

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Removes `window.relocationJobs` and `events.js` permanently | ~8–10 weeks; large parallel track |
| One design system, one test strategy | Regression risk during migration (mitigated below) |
| Professional mobile + dark/light in one place | Requires Flask SPA fallback route |

### D. Full rewrite + TypeScript

Same as C but port to `.tsx` during migration.

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Stronger types long-term | ~30–50% more migration friction; defer until SPA is stable |

---

## Decision: scope

| Choice | When |
|--------|------|
| **C — Full SPA** | Owner wants production-grade UI, mobile, themes, and maintainability; accepts 8–10 week track |
| **A — Polish only** | Reliability/backend work is higher priority; UI refresh is cosmetic |
| **B — Gradual React** | Want smaller PRs but eventually reach SPA |

**Owner preference (2026-07-08):** full SPA + dark/light themes.

---

## Recommended stack

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|-------|--------|-----|
| **Router** | React Router v7 | Mirrors `/`, `/company/:country/:slug`, `/apply`, `/admin` |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS v4 + CSS variables | Token-based theming; component-scoped utility |
| **Primitives** | shadcn/ui (Radix) | Dialog, Sheet, Select, Dropdown, Toast — accessible defaults |
| **Server state** | TanStack Query | Board pagination, admin tables, fetch runs |
| **Client state** | Zustand (light) | Auth session, filter prefs (`storage.js` today), theme override |
| **Icons** | lucide-react | Replace duplicated inline SVGs |
| **Build** | Existing Vite → `relocation_jobs/static/dist/` | Deploy path unchanged |

---

## Design direction

### Visual language

- **Typography:** 12 / 14 / 16 / 20 / 24px scale; Inter (UI), JetBrains Mono (slugs/code).
- **Spacing:** 4px base grid (4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48).
- **Surfaces:** three elevation levels (page → card → popover); subtle borders over heavy shadows.
- **Color:** semantic tokens for both themes (`background`, `foreground`, `muted`, `accent`, `success`, `warn`, `danger`, `visa`, `referral`); remove raw hex in badges.
- **Density:** comfortable desktop; 44px min touch targets on `pointer: coarse`.

### Dark + light theme

- `data-theme="dark" | "light"` on `<html>`.
- Default from `prefers-color-scheme`; override in `localStorage`.
- Theme toggle in account menu; inline anti-FOUC script in SPA shell.
- Verify contrast for ATS rings, visa/referral chips, and badges in both modes.

### Mobile-first layout

- **AppShell:** sticky header, safe-area insets, max-width columns (1080px board, 1280px workspace).
- **Board filters:** desktop inline toolbar; mobile bottom **Sheet** with chip summary (replace `lockBodyScroll` + manual backdrop).
- **Job actions:** wrap to 2-col at `md`, 1-col at `sm`.
- **Company workspace:** sidebar + detail → stacked below `lg`.
- **Admin tables:** horizontal scroll + sticky first column; optional card list later.

### UX polish

- Skeleton loaders per page (extend `BoardSkeleton.jsx`).
- Empty states with clear CTAs.
- Optimistic updates via TanStack Query `onMutate` / `setQueryData` (port `job-board.js` logic).
- `prefers-reduced-motion` for sheets and loaders.
- Focus traps on Dialog/Sheet; `focus-visible` on all interactives.

---

## Target architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph today [Today]
    HTML[4 HTML shells]
    VAN[static/js ~29 modules]
    REACT[frontend React island]
    CSS[styles.css monolith]
    HTML --> VAN
    HTML --> REACT
    VAN <-->|window.relocationJobs| REACT
    VAN --> API
    REACT --> API
  end

  subgraph target [Target]
    SPA[Single React SPA]
    DS[Design system + themes]
    HOOKS[API hooks + client state]
    SPA --> DS
    SPA --> HOOKS
    HOOKS --> API2[Flask /api/*]
  end
```

### Frontend layout (new)

```text
frontend/src/
  app/           App.jsx, router, providers (Query, theme, auth)
  layouts/       AppShell, AuthGate
  pages/         BoardPage, CompanyPage, ApplyPage, AdminPage
  features/      board/, company/, apply/, admin/, fetch/, auth/
  components/ui/ shadcn primitives
  lib/           api client (from api.js), query keys, formatters
  hooks/         useBoard, useJobMutations, useTheme, …
  styles/        globals.css (theme tokens), tailwind config
```

### Flask changes (minimal)

In `relocation_jobs/web/server.py`:

- Serve **one** SPA shell for `/`, `/admin`, `/apply`, `/company/<country>/<path:slug>`.
- Keep `/static/dist/*` and `/api/*` unchanged.
- Retire per-page HTML routes after each phase.

### Delete after cutover

- `relocation_jobs/static/*.html` (except SPA shell)
- `relocation_jobs/static/js/*` (entire vanilla layer)
- `relocation_jobs/static/styles.css`
- `window.relocationJobs` bridge (`board-sync.js`, `fetch-sync.js`)

---

## Phased migration (~8–10 weeks)

Each phase ends deployable; production stays usable throughout.

### Phase 0 — Foundation (week 1–2)

- Add Tailwind, shadcn/ui, React Router, TanStack Query, lucide-react.
- Theme tokens (dark/light); `AppShell` with header, user menu, theme toggle, nav.
- Port `api.js` → `lib/api.js`; `auth.js` → `AuthGate`.
- Flask SPA fallback; Vite dev proxy to `:5051`.
- **Deliverable:** authenticated shell with theme toggle; placeholder routes.

### Phase 1 — Job board (week 3–5)

| Today | Target |
|-------|--------|
| `filters.js` + HTML toolbar | `BoardToolbar` + filter Sheet |
| `board.js`, `board-view.js` | `useBoardQuery` + pagination |
| `events.js` delegated clicks | `useJobMutations` in `JobCard` / `CompanyCard` |
| `dialogs.js` (~1,154 lines) | `AddCompanyDialog`, `EditCareersDialog`, `EditCityDialog` |
| `FetchPanel.jsx` + `scrape.js` | `useFetchRun`; single fetch UI |
| `job-board.js` optimistic patches | Query `onMutate` / `setQueryData` |

Refactor existing React components to **own interactions** (no `data-action` delegation).

**Deliverable:** `/` fully functional in SPA; legacy board JS removable.

### Phase 2 — Company workspace (week 6–7)

Port `company.js` (~621 lines): position list, LaTeX editor, PDF preview, MCP actions. Route `/company/:country/:slug`. Merge duplicate `companyWorkspace.js`.

**Deliverable:** CV tailoring in SPA.

### Phase 3 — Application data (week 8)

Port `apply.js`: profile form, master resume editor, pipeline prompts. Optional React Hook Form.

**Deliverable:** `/apply` in SPA.

### Phase 4 — Admin (week 9–10)

Port `admin.js`, `admin-worker.js`, `stats-dashboard.js`. Reuse fetch panel from Phase 1.

**Deliverable:** `/admin` in SPA; delete legacy static JS/HTML.

### Phase 5 — Polish (final week)

- Accessibility audit (keyboard, focus traps, labels).
- Mobile QA (iOS Safari safe areas, sheets).
- Route-based code splitting (`React.lazy`).
- Bundle size / lucide tree-shaking.
- Update `architecture.md`; adjust deploy if SPA shell name changes.

---

## Testing strategy

### Do not do

- Retroactive unit/e2e tests for legacy `static/js/` or the React↔vanilla bridge.
- Block Phase 0 on front test coverage.

**Rationale:** legacy front is throwaway (~8,500 LOC). Backend pytest already guards API routes (`tests/test_route_manifest.py`), panel flatten (`tests/panel/`), and business rules. The SPA rewrite **improves** testability by colocating behavior in hooks/components.

### Do during migration

| Layer | Tool | Scope | When |
|-------|------|-------|------|
| Pure logic | Vitest | `format.js`, `sort.js`, optimistic cache helpers | Phase 0–1 |
| Critical flows | Playwright (3–5 tests) | Login → board load; apply toggle persists; filter + paginate; optional company workspace | After Phase 1 |
| API contract | pytest (existing) | Route manifest, board flatten, mutations | Every phase |

### Playwright smoke list (minimum)

1. Login → board renders companies.
2. Toggle applied on a job → persists after reload.
3. Change filter + page → correct slice shown.
4. (Optional) Open company workspace → positions list loads.

### Regression checklist (manual + Playwright)

Map to [business-rules.md](business-rules.md): apply, reject, not-for-me, restore, pin, ATS score, hide-reason, orphan reinjection visible on board.

---

## Risks and mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| `events.js` behavior regression | Migrate action-by-action; checklist vs business-rules |
| Optimistic board updates wrong bucket | Port `patchJobOnBoard` into query cache helpers before deleting `job-board.js` |
| Fetch polling stuck / double poll | Isolate `useFetchPolling`; mock poll in Vitest |
| 6k-line CSS delete | Rebuild with shadcn + tokens; port only ATS ring, visa badges |
| Long admin migration | Admin last; board + company = 90% daily use |
| Deploy breakage | SPA shell first; optional feature flag for old HTML one release |
| Parallel backend reliability work | UI rewrite does not fix board load perf — coordinate with [board-read-model-proposal.md](board-read-model-proposal.md) |

---

## What “done” looks like

- One Vite build → `relocation_jobs/static/dist/`.
- Four React Router routes; shared AppShell on every page.
- Dark + light themes; system default + manual override.
- Mobile: usable filters, job actions, company workspace without horizontal scroll pain.
- No `window.relocationJobs`, no `events.js`, no duplicate fetch panel.
- API and business rules unchanged; pytest green; Playwright smoke green.

---

## Suggested first PR

1. Tooling + theme tokens + `AppShell` + Flask SPA fallback.
2. Login/auth gate against existing `/api/auth/*`.
3. Board route renders existing React `App` inside new layout (proves integration before deleting vanilla).

Visible progress on day one without big-bang cutover.

---

## Open questions

1. **Timing vs board performance** — start SPA while [board-read-model-proposal.md](board-read-model-proposal.md) is in flight, or sequence after read-model lands?
2. **Feature flag** — keep old HTML shells for one release behind env flag?
3. **React Hook Form** — adopt in Phase 3 apply page or stay uncontrolled + manual validation?
4. **Admin table density** — horizontal scroll only, or card fallback on mobile in v1?

The doc covers:

- **Summary** — full SPA rewrite, dark/light themes, mobile-first design system
- **Current pain points** — hybrid React/vanilla bridge, monolithic CSS, duplication
- **Options A–D** — polish-only vs gradual React vs full SPA (your choice: full SPA)
- **Recommended stack** — React Router, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, TanStack Query, Zustand
- **Design direction** — tokens, typography, mobile sheets, UX polish
- **Phased migration** — Phases 0–5 (~8–10 weeks)
- **Testing strategy** — no legacy front tests; Vitest + Playwright smoke on the new stack
- **Risks, done criteria, first PR scope, open questions**

Ready for your next question.

content.text:
Applied. Summary of changes:

### Typography tokens (`styles.css`)
- Added `--text-xs` through `--text-xl`, line-height, tracking, spacing, and motion tokens to `:root`
- Body uses `--text-base` and `--leading-normal`
- `.app-title`, `h1`/`.page-title`, company names, job titles, badges, and stat values now use the scale

### UX polish
- Global `:focus-visible` rings on interactive elements
- `prefers-reduced-motion` disables animations/transitions
- Semantic utilities: `.text-xs`, `.text-sm`, `.text-muted`, `.text-label`, `.tabular-nums`
- Touch targets raised to **2.75rem (44px)** on coarse pointers for header buttons, selects, and job action buttons
- Header/card transitions use `--duration-fast` / `--ease-out`

### Empty states
- [`App.jsx`](frontend/src/App.jsx) — structured title + hint
- [`CompanyCard.jsx`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx) — clearer empty-company message; careers link uses `.job-title--secondary` (inline style removed)

### Cache busting
- All four HTML shells now use `styles.css?v=52`
- `board.js?v=3` on index (rebuilt)

Hard refresh locally (`Cmd+Shift+R`) to see it. For production: `./scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh sync`.

content.text:
Fixed — the branding CSS was clipping dropdowns and popovers.

**Root cause:** `overflow: hidden` on `.app-header` and `.admin-header` cut off the account menu, scrape settings, and filter panels that extend outside the header card.

**Also fixed:**
- Raised z-index on dropdowns/popovers so they sit above the header
- `pointer-events: none` on decorative gradient bars (so they don’t steal clicks)
- Removed card hover `transform` (could shift click targets)
- Home-page brand is no longer a link (avoids accidental full reload)
- Toolbar/filter bar set to `overflow: visible`

Hard refresh at **http://127.0.0.1:5051** (`Cmd+Shift+R`) — need `styles.css?v=54`.

Try account menu, filters, job actions (apply/reject), and add company. If something’s still off, say which control and I’ll trace it.

content.text:
Two issues in your screenshot:

1. **Double focus ring** on the country select — global `outline` + `box-shadow` stacked. Fixed to a single ring on form controls.
2. **Cramped single-row header** — at ~1080px width, brand + 3 filters + 4 buttons in one row squeezed "Germany" against the chevron. Reverted to **two rows**: brand on top, filters + actions below, with wider dropdowns and more chevron padding.

Hard refresh for `styles.css?v=56`. The header should breathe properly and focus states should look clean.

[REDACTED]
